skill.name: maxhub-wechat
skill.description: WeChat data query assistant covering Channels and Official Accounts with search, video details, comments, articles, and user analysis.
skill.summary: Query WeChat Channels video content and Official Account articles through natural language.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 370 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives from pattern matching on markdown API documentation. The 221 backtick detections are JSON Path expressions and code spans in reference docs (not shell execution). The 54 hardcoded URL detections point to legitimate service URLs (aconfig.cn, weixin.qq.com). The 16 env_access detections are confirmed but legitimate: the skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY for authorized API access. The 58 weak cryptographic algorithm detections are JSON field names like decode_key and url_token in API documentation, not actual crypto implementation. Overall the skill is a legitimate WeChat data API client with transparent behavior. It makes curl-based API calls to aconfig.cn using a user-provided API key. All critical and high blocking findings are dismissed as false positives from documentation content.

medium_findings.title: Confirmed env_access: API key in environment variable
medium_findings.description: Skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for API authentication. This is legitimate and transparently documented in SKILL.md and README files. The API key is passed as a Bearer token header in all curl requests to aconfig.cn. Users should ensure their API key is stored securely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: MAXHUB_API_KEY is explicitly declared in SKILL.md metadata as a required env var with the 'sensitive: true' flag. Usage pattern is standard Bearer token auth.

medium_findings.title: curl-based API access pattern
medium_findings.description: Skill uses curl commands with Bearer token authentication to call the external MaxHub API. This is the intended functionality for querying WeChat data. The API base URL (aconfig.cn) is hardcoded and transparent. No evidence of unauthorized data exfiltration or communication with unexpected endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All curl commands in SKILL.md reference the same base URL (aconfig.cn) with documented endpoints. The API key is used as a Bearer token, which is a standard and expected authentication pattern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation (false positive - legitimate references)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 54 hardcoded URLs. All URLs point to the service provider (aconfig.cn), WeChat article links (mp.weixin.qq.com), or open-source tools on GitHub. These are legitimate documentation references for the skill's API integration. No malicious or unexpected network destinations were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network references reviewed: aconfig.cn is the skill's API provider, weixin.qq.com is WeChat's domain, and GitHub URLs point to open-source decryption tools referenced in documentation.

low_findings.title: Backtick content in documentation (false positive - not shell execution)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 221 backtick patterns as Ruby/shell execution. These are actually markdown code spans and fenced code blocks containing API endpoint paths, JSON Path expressions, parameter names, and documentation examples. No actual system command execution was found beyond documented curl usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are in markdown .md files. Content includes JSON Path expressions like $.data.media_list[*], API paths like POST /api/v1/wechat_channels/..., and parameter names like url_token. None are executable commands.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection (false positive - API field names)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged references to decode_key, url_token, last_buffer, and code-like patterns as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are JSON response field names in API documentation describing WeChat video decryption metadata. The skill does not implement or invoke any cryptographic functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are in API reference documentation describing WeChat API response structures. Field names like decode_key and url_token are JSON keys returned by the API, not cryptographic implementations in the skill.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detection (false positive - JSON Path expressions)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged JSON Path expressions as system reconnaissance. Patterns like $.data.object_list[-1].last_buffer are data navigation expressions used to document API response structures, not system file access or directory traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Flagged content consists of standard JSON Path expressions inside markdown code spans in API reference documentation. They navigate JSON response structures, not file system paths.

low_findings.title: High file entropy detection (false positive - mixed language content)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged api-channels.md (618 lines) for high entropy, suggesting possible binary/encrypted content. The file contains structured API documentation with mixed Chinese and English text, code spans, and JSON Path expressions. No obfuscated or encrypted content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is fully readable markdown with bilingual API documentation. High entropy is caused by the mix of Chinese characters, English text, special characters in JSON Path expressions, and repeated structured patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: API client with env var credential and network access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines environment variable access (MAXHUB_API_KEY), network requests (curl to aconfig.cn), and external commands (curl execution) to function as a third-party API client. While this is the skill's intended purpose, the combination of credential access and network calls means a compromised API provider could access the API key scope.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed: the skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from env, passes it as Bearer token in curl requests to aconfig.cn. All code is transparent and documented. Risk is inherent to any third-party API client pattern.

content.user_title: Query WeChat Data for Channels and Official Accounts
content.value_statement: Searching WeChat video channels and public accounts requires complex API integrations. This skill lets you query video details, comments, articles, and user data through simple natural language prompts.
actual_capabilities: Search and analyze WeChat Channels video content with details, comments, and engagement metrics
Query Official Account articles with full text, metadata, and read counts
Search for users, videos, and content by keywords across WeChat Channels and Official Accounts
Compare and analyze multiple data points across channels and time periods side by side
Handle paginated results and follow-up queries like next page or deeper analysis
Support bilingual Chinese and English natural language queries
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn to function. No key means no data access.
Some WeChat video files use encryption and cannot be played directly without external decryption tools.
Data accuracy depends on the third-party MaxHub API provider and may have update delays.
Rate limits apply based on your API key tier. Excessive requests may be throttled.

use_cases.title: Competitor Content Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track competitor Official Account publishing frequency, content themes, and article read performance over time to optimize your own content strategy.

use_cases.title: Video Channel Performance Analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze WeChat Channels video views, engagement metrics, comments, and follower growth to measure content effectiveness and guide optimization.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: WeChat Ecosystem Market Research
use_cases.description: Research content strategies across the WeChat ecosystem by searching and analyzing top accounts, trending topics, and audience engagement patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Search for WeChat Channels videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for videos about artificial intelligence on WeChat Channels. Show me the top 10 results with view counts and publish dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner user wants to find video content on a topic of interest.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a specific video channel
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the WeChat video channel named TechReview. Get their home page information and list their latest 20 videos with view counts. Compare the most viewed video against the average.
prompt_templates.scenario: An intermediate user wants to evaluate a specific channel's performance.

prompt_templates.title: Deep dive into Official Account articles
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Official Account named AI Daily. Fetch their article list, then get the full details and read counts for the 5 most recent articles. Summarize the content themes and engagement metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: A content analyst wants to study an account's publishing patterns in depth.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-module competitive analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for content about electric vehicles across both WeChat Channels and Official Accounts. For Channels, find the top 10 videos with engagement data. For Official Accounts, find the top 5 articles with read counts. Compare which format generates more engagement.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants a comprehensive multi-platform analysis on a topic.

output_examples.input: Search for videos about cooking on WeChat Channels
output_examples.output:
Found 15 videos about cooking on WeChat Channels:

1. **Quick 5-Minute Pasta Recipe** - 120K views, 2.3K likes
2. **Beginner Sushi Tutorial** - 89K views, 1.8K likes
3. **Healthy Breakfast Ideas** - 67K views, 1.2K likes
...

Top channel: **HomeChef** with 450K total subscribers.

Would you like me to analyze any of these videos in more detail or see comments?

output_examples.input: Analyze the WeChat Official Account named TechInsights
output_examples.output:
**TechInsights** - Official Account Analysis:

Recent Articles (last 5):
| Title | Reads | Date |
|-------|-------|------|
| AI in 2025: What Changed | 85K | Mar 15 |
| Smartphone Market Trends | 62K | Mar 12 |
| Cloud Computing Guide | 48K | Mar 10 |

The account posts 3-4 times per week with high engagement on technical analysis pieces.

best_practices: Start with a specific search to confirm the API key is working before running complex multi-endpoint queries.
Use the browse mode for quick lookups and the analyze mode for detailed reports with tables and comparisons.
Ask for the next page of results when you need more data rather than repeating the same search.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for raw API response JSON. The skill processes API responses into readable summaries automatically.
Do not request API keys or credentials through the conversation. Configure the MAXHUB_API_KEY in your environment before starting.
Do not expect real-time data. The MaxHub API may have caching delays. Results reflect the most recent data available from the API provider.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn. Register an account, create an API key in your dashboard, and set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can I search both Channels and Official Accounts at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports cross-module searches. You can ask for content from both Channels and Official Accounts in a single request.

faq.question: Why can I not play some video files directly?
faq.answer: WeChat encrypts some Channels video files. The API provides a decode_key field and links to third-party decryption tools for those files.

faq.question: Does this skill work in English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports both Chinese and English. It detects your language and responds in the same language.

faq.question: What happens when I ask for the next page of results?
faq.answer: The skill automatically uses the pagination parameters from the previous response to fetch the next batch of results without needing to re-enter search terms.

faq.question: Is my API key safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: The skill uses your API key only as a Bearer token header when calling MaxHub API endpoints. The SKILL.md explicitly instructs the AI to keep API key values out of all outputs.
